sessions.details: The line-up was Peter Cox on vocals; Chris West on guitar; Colin Pattenden on bass; Chris Slade on drums; and Dave Fishel on keyboards.

track_listing.track: I Don’t Want to Go On without You

track_listing.details: LP - The Smoker You Drink, the Player You Get

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. Thomas the Vance here, TV on the radio, and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
Tonight we have four archive sets by that bum supergroup, the Moody Blues, including their first ever broadcast,
and a session from Terranova, made up of five musicians who've been playing in other bands for quite some time and know what they're doing.
In the new release area, we've got three tracks from No Place to Run.
That's the next UFO album due out in January.
Also new releases by Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers and Night and Ian North.
Classic cuts tonight, The Rolling Stones, Joe Walsh and Motorhead.
And others, which you'll find in the Friday Night Connection.
But tonight we start with Kiss.

And this is Patrick Lunt in the Midnight Newsroom.
The headlines, President Carter proposes international economic sanctions against Iran.
After three months of talking, the Rhodesia Agreement is signed.
And the national steel strike is still on, despite a new offer.
President Carter has decided to ask the United Nations to approve worldwide economic sanctions against Iran
in an effort to force the regime in Tehran to release the 50 American hostages.
Speaking at the White House tonight, Mr. Carter said the time for action had come.
The government of Iran must realize that it cannot flaunt with impunity
the expressed will and law of the world community.
The Security Council must act to enforce its demand that Iran release the hostages.
And the United States is dispatching the giant nuclear aircraft carrier USS Nimitz
and two guided missile cruisers to the waters off Iran.
They'll be relieving the carrier USS Kitty Hawk, which is returning to California.
The Nimitz is in the Mediterranean at present,
and will be sailing around the Cape of Good Hope to reach the Arabian Sea by the end of January.
After the signing in London of the peace agreement for Rhodesia,
the United Nations Security Council has lifted its 13-year economic embargo against the country.
And in Rhodesia itself, the British governor, Lord Soames,
has now removed the ban on the political activities of the Patriotic Front.
and Lord Soames has been speaking on Rhodesian television
about his hopes for the future of Rhodesia.
